title: External human approval boundary
outOfScope: Creating synthetic platform approvals or treating Contract approval as a GitHub review

sources.reason: Repository-specific CODEOWNERS entry for Required Review.
Document the external platform approval boundary.

riskAssessment.reason: CODEOWNERS is repository evidence; Required Review is external platform evidence.

executionDecision.reason: Repository ownership is explicit; platform review remains independently evidenced.

preReviewWarnings: Verify CODEOWNERS resolution and branch protection outside the repository.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: CODEOWNERS names the real repository owner and contains no placeholder token.
Workflow documentation distinguishes Contract approval from platform Required Review.
Automated checks pass and the Work Item evidence records the external protection result.
The Work Item is archived and its PR is merged before lifecycle closure.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: Repository-local Contract approval can be mistaken for a human platform review.
constraints: Do not fabricate or bypass review evidence.
intent.rationale: Make ownership explicit in CODEOWNERS and document the independent platform boundary.

evidence: Repository workflow states the two are independent.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
